Off Calendar Nov-21-2025 Continued To Feb-24-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Points And Auth.; Declaration
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's unopposed Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is GRANTED.

Based on its review of the record, the court finds the weight of the evidence shows Plaintiff did not personally serve the complaint on Defendant Colletti. (See Colletti Dec. at p. 6; Code of Civil Procedure section 415.10.) Nor did Plaintiff accomplish substitute service. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 415.20.) The motion is granted on the independent grounds that the court lacks personal jurisdiction over Defendant because the record is devoid of evidence that Plaintiff properly served a summons on Defendant. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 413.10 et seq.)

Service on Defendant Stephen Colletti and Plaintiff's Proof of Service-Civil, filed Mar. 23, 2023, is of no effect.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving Party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Moving Party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar Sep-18-2025 Continued To Nov-25-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2025 Continued To Oct-07-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss With Prejudice
Off Calendar Jun-26-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DDefendant Stephen Colletti's unopposed Motion To Dismiss With Prejudice is OFF CALENDAR.

Defendant failed timely to file proof of service of his moving papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) The court will not consider the merits of an unopposed motion absent timely submission of proof that the moving papers were properly served.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MINI MINUTES: Kenneth Tsang, Esq. specially appearing for the plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to June 25, 2024 at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: SAMUEL K. FENG; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-21-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Defendant STEPHEN TODD COLLETTI (Tel: 628-213-4498) in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT STEPHEN COLLETTI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Specially appearing Defendant's unopposed motion to quash service of summons is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The court cannot consider this motion on the merits because Defendant failed to submit adequate proof of service of the moving papers. For example, service upon Plaintiff is purported to be electronic, but the record does not establish that Plaintiff consented to electronic service. (See Cal. Rules Ct., rule 2.251(b).) Even if electronic service upon Plaintiff were proper, Defendant's proof of service does not identify the electronic address of the person served. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1031b, subd. (4).) Further, the proof of service is unsigned and not sworn to under penalty of perjury. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2015.5.)

Order signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
